findings.message: get_stock now hard-depends on data["reserved_count"], but the consumer pact in tests/functional/test_consumer_inventory.py declares only {item_id, in_stock} in with_body(...). The consumer contract therefore fails to pin a field the consumer actually requires: provider verification can pass while the provider omits reserved_count, and the real client will KeyError at runtime. This is exactly the gap the pact exists to catch — the contract under-specifies the consumer's true expectation (the inline comment even admits the field is 'not declared in the pact interaction').
findings.suggestion: Add reserved_count to the pact interaction so the contract pins it: .with_body({"item_id": match.int(1), "in_stock": match.int(5), "reserved_count": match.int(0)}), then regenerate/publish pacts/demo-inventory.json. (The consumer test's `== 5` assertion must also be updated for the new dict return, and will currently fail against the mock since the body lacks reserved_count.)
